name: remittance_fraud_model_worker_payday_block
premise: Payments integrity lead Casey Jones must decide whether to tighten a fraud model that blocks same-day remittances when sender velocity spikes, a pattern that catches scam rings but also blocks migrant workers sending lump sums on shared payday Wi-Fi at one storefront. Engineer Lina Webb shows false positives cluster on specific corridor routes. Loosening the model may let through mule accounts laundering for human traffickers flagged by FinCEN. Keeping it may strand rent for hundreds tonight.

agents.name: Casey Jones
agents.identity: I am the payments integrity lead balancing FinCEN alerts, consumer protection, and equitable access for corridor remittance users.
agents.goal: I want fraud blocked without geographically over-policing legitimate payday clustering patterns.

agents.name: Lina Webb
agents.identity: I am the engineer who tuned velocity features and documented corridor false positives against ground-truth interviews.
agents.goal: I want velocity rules loosened with voucher-based identity uplift for storefront senders even if review queues spike.

choices: I will tighten the model and deploy pop-up verification booths at known corridor stores tonight, accepting long lines and some trafficking risk if booths are understaffed.
I will loosen velocity thresholds and shift to beneficiary-behavior subgraph scoring, accepting higher fraud loss while unblocking lump-sum payday sends.
forced_choice.call_to_action: FinCEN escalated a trafficking typology an hour ago. Casey Jones must choose between tightening with booths or loosening velocity with new subgraph scoring. What does Casey Jones do?
